evals.prompt: Generate Storybook documentation stories for the Button component. The source files are in the button-component/ directory: Button.tsx, Button.types.ts, Button.css, and index.ts. The helpers are in stories/helpers/. Output the docs story file as Button.docs.stories.tsx.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive CSF3 documentation story file with Default, Sentiments, Emphases, Sizes, States, AllCombinations, TokenReference, Anatomy, UsageGuidance, and DarkMode stories.
expectations: Output file uses CSF3 format with 'satisfies Meta<typeof Button>'
Meta has tags: ['autodocs'] and layout: 'fullscreen'
All 5 sentiments from types file appear in a Sentiments story (neutral, warning, highlight, success, error)
All 3 emphasis levels appear in an Emphases story (high, medium, low)
All 5 sizes appear in a Sizes story (xs, sm, md, lg, xl)
All 5 states appear in a States story (rest, hover, active, disabled, resolving)
An AllCombinations story renders sentiment × emphasis matrix
A TokenReference story with token array extracted from CSS custom properties
Token categories include color, spacing, typography, and structure
An Anatomy story with numbered elements matching JSX structure (root, prefix, label, suffix, spinner)
A UsageGuidance story using DosDonts helper
A DarkMode story showing light and dark side by side
Imports use helpers from stories/helpers/ (DocPage, TokenTable, DosDonts, etc.)
argTypes are grouped into Dimensions, Content, and Events categories
No hardcoded color or spacing values — all derived from source files
Structure dimension (standard, icon-only, split) has a corresponding story or is represented

evals.prompt: Create documentation stories for our Button component in Storybook. I need the full docs treatment — all the dimension grids, token table, anatomy breakdown, and usage do/don'ts. Component files are in button-component/.
evals.expected_output: Same comprehensive output but triggered by a more casual prompt. Should still produce all required sections.
expectations: Output file uses CSF3 format with 'satisfies Meta<typeof Button>'
Meta has tags: ['autodocs'] and layout: 'fullscreen'
All 5 sentiments from types file appear in a story
All 3 emphasis levels appear in a story
All 5 sizes appear in a story
All 5 states appear in a story
Token table extracts CSS custom properties from the CSS file — not invented values
Anatomy matches the JSX structure (btn root, btn__prefix, btn__label, btn__suffix, btn__spinner)
Uses DosDonts helper for usage guidance
Structure dimension is represented (standard, icon-only, split)
No hardcoded color or spacing values

evals.prompt: Add docs stories for Button. Just follow the storybook documentation skill.
evals.expected_output: Even with a minimal prompt, the skill should guide comprehensive output with all required sections.
expectations: Output file is named Button.docs.stories.tsx
Uses CSF3 format with proper TypeScript types
Contains a Default story rendering the component at defaults
Contains dimension stories for all 5 dimensions the component uses
Contains a token reference section
Contains an anatomy section
Contains usage guidance
All dimension modes come from the types file, not hardcoded guesses
CSS custom properties in token table match what's actually in Button.css
